tasks.spec:
You are a fix worker on the loop-factory repo. Your current working directory IS a git worktree of /mnt/d_drive/repos/loop-factory - edit files here directly. Do NOT git commit, branch, or push; leave changes uncommitted. Do not load skills or call MCP/Apps. No network access. Python stdlib only.

CONTEXT: the podcast watchdog's incidents (state/incidents.json, dict keyed by 12-char fingerprint of sensor|subject) currently never leave state 'open': after a noisy shadow run, 16 threads stay open while only 7 candidates are current. The charter's escalate-once rule depends on incidents reaching 'resolved' so that RECURRENCE can be detected (resolved fingerprint recurring -> department_defect). Missing piece: auto-resolve on sustained healthy observation.

OWNERSHIP: you own ONLY departments/podcast/runtime/fingerprint_dedup.py and departments/podcast/tests/test_watchdog_spine.py. Everything else is frozen; the module already receives the full observation list (or can load observations.jsonl from --state-dir) plus candidates.

FIX (in fingerprint_dedup.py):
1. Each cycle, for every OPEN or DEPARTMENT_DEFECT incident whose subject+sensor has a current observation with status 'ok' AND no candidate this cycle: increment an incident field consecutive_healthy (default 0). Any cycle where the subject has a candidate, a non-ok observation, or no observation at all resets consecutive_healthy to 0.
2. When consecutive_healthy reaches 3: set state='resolved', add resolved_at (ISO from the newest observation ts, never wall-clock-free invention), resolution='observed_healthy_3_cycles', keep escalated flag and full history fields intact. A resolved incident later recurring must still follow the existing rule (reopen as department_defect).
3. Idempotent and atomic as before; no changes to fingerprint computation, migration, escalated semantics, or any other module.
4. Tests (test_watchdog_spine.py): add (a) three consecutive healthy cycles resolve an open incident and preserve escalated; (b) a candidate on cycle 2 resets the counter (still open after two more healthy cycles, resolves on the third); (c) resolved-then-recurring still becomes department_defect; (d) subject with NO observation does not accrue healthy cycles. Keep all existing tests passing.

HOW TO RUN: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m pytest departments/podcast/tests/ -q

OUTPUT CONTRACT: leave changes uncommitted; write ./fix-summary.md ('# Fix Summary', '## Summary', '## Files Changed', '## Verification', '## Assumptions', <700 words).
tasks.verified: full department pytest passed including resolve-after-3-healthy, counter-reset, recurrence-after-resolve, and no-observation tests
